home.title: Smart Agriculture
home.subtitle: Starting Simply
home.description1: PlantGuard protects your crops with advanced AI.
home.description2: Early detection of pathologies and real-time monitoring of field health.
home.monitor_desc: Analyze data from Drones/Cameras to early detect stressed areas on a large scale.
home.doctor_desc: Accurately diagnose leaf diseases and receive immediate treatment protocols.
home.footer: © 2026 PlantGuard AI • Smart crop protection solutions

monitor.title: Garden Monitoring
monitor.subtitle: Analyze Live Cam & Drone data in real-time.
monitor.processing_desc: The system is scanning data. You can visit other pages while waiting.
monitor.disclaimer: Note: Analysis results are based on remote imagery. In low light or shaky camera conditions, accuracy may decrease. Please cross-reference with Plant Doctor data for the most accurate results.
monitor.camera_desc: Enter URL and press Start to scan the field
monitor.error_upload: Error analyzing video. Please try again.
monitor.error_reconnecting: Connection interrupted. Attempting to reconnect automatically...

doctor.title: Plant Doctor
doctor.subtitle: Diagnose pathogens through artificial intelligence.
doctor.recommendation_desc: This technique is based on deep learning models. Results may vary depending on image quality. Please consult an expert before applying large-scale plant protection chemicals.
doctor.server_error: Server encountered an issue processing the image. Please try again later.

history.title: Diagnosis History
history.subtitle: Review all your crop diagnosis records

Apple Healthy.name: Healthy Apple Leaf
Apple Healthy.desc: Apple leaf in its best growth state.

treatments.identification_guide: Leaf maintains smooth green color, no abnormal signs found.
Entire canopy looks uniform, no abnormal yellow leaf drop.
treatments.action: Regularly check for pests on the underside of leaves

treatments.identification_guide: Growth points develop well, healthy young shoots.
treatments.action: Maintain macro, medium, and micro-nutrient fertilization regime

Apple Scab.name: Apple Scab
Apple Scab.desc: Disease caused by the fungus Venturia inaequalis, severely affecting apple yield.

treatments.identification_guide: A few small olive-colored spots appear on the underside of leaves.
treatments.action: Clean up fallen leaves, water at the base

treatments.identification_guide: Many brown-black spots enlarge on both sides of the leaf, leaf begins to curl slightly.
Dense ulcers make leaves wrinkled, dry, and fall early in mass.

Apple Black Rot.name: Apple Black Rot
Apple Black Rot.desc: Fungal disease Diplodia seriata causing dark rot spots.

treatments.identification_guide: Small purple spots appear on the upper leaf surface.
Spots enlarge and develop a 'frog-eye' appearance with a tan center.
Leaves turn yellow and drop, fruit shows black rot, branch cankers expand.

Apple Cedar Rust.name: Cedar Apple Rust
Apple Cedar Rust.desc: Gymnosporangium juniperi-virginianae fungus causes prominent orange spots.
symptoms: Bright orange spots on the upper leaf surface
Small fungal spikes under the leaf

treatments.identification_guide: A few small lemon-yellow spots appear individually on the leaf surface.
Spots turn dark orange, small black dots begin to appear in the center of the lesion.
treatments.action: Spray when young leaves first emerge

treatments.identification_guide: Spore-bearing spike-like tubes grow under the leaf surface, leaves turn yellow and fall.

Blueberry Healthy.name: Healthy Blueberry Leaf
Blueberry Healthy.desc: Analysis of blueberry leaf status in good growth.

treatments.identification_guide: Leaves are even green, no signs of insect sucking.

Cherry Powdery Mildew.name: Cherry Powdery Mildew
Cherry Powdery Mildew.desc: Disease caused by Podosphaera clandestina fungus causing a white powder layer on leaves.
symptoms: White powder layer on leaf surface

treatments.identification_guide: Faint white spots appear on the underside of young leaves.
White powder covers 30-50% of leaf area, leaf margins slightly curled.
treatments.action: Spray baking soda or sulfur solution

treatments.identification_guide: Leaves completely covered by powder, dry brittle and drop.
Healthy leaves, no signs of powdery mildew.

Corn Common Rust.name: Corn Common Rust
Corn Common Rust.desc: Disease caused by Puccinia sorghi fungus, reddish-brown pustules appear.
symptoms: Cinnamon-brown pustules on both leaf surfaces

treatments.identification_guide: A few brown pustules scattered on the lower leaves.
Pustules appear more and spread to upper leaves.
treatments.action: Spray leaf fungicide if moist conditions

treatments.identification_guide: Leaves covered by pustules, turn yellow and die dry.

Corn Gray Leaf Spot.name: Corn Gray Leaf Spot
Corn Gray Leaf Spot.desc: Disease caused by Cercospora zeae-maydis fungus causing rectangular lesions.
symptoms: Gray rectangular lesions running parallel to leaf veins

treatments.identification_guide: Small tan spots surrounded by a yellow halo.
Lesions lengthen into gray rectangles 1-2 cm long.
Lesions connect to each other killing the whole leaf.

Corn Northern Leaf Blight.name: Northern Corn Leaf Blight
Corn Northern Leaf Blight.desc: Disease caused by Exserohilum turcicum fungus causing spindle-shaped lesions.
symptoms: Large gray-green or brown spindle-shaped lesions
Often appears in cool and moist weather

treatments.identification_guide: A few small cigar-shaped lesions on lower leaves.
Long spindle lesions appear on many leaves.
Leaves completely blighted, severe impact on grain.

Corn Healthy.name: Healthy Corn Leaf
symptoms: No signs of fungus or rust

treatments.identification_guide: Healthy leaves, no spots or brown pustules.
treatments.action: Ensure adequate nitrogen and water supply

Grape Black Rot.name: Grape Black Rot
Grape Black Rot.desc: Disease caused by Guignardia bidwellii fungus seriously damaging grapes.
symptoms: Circular brown spots on leaves with small black dots

treatments.identification_guide: A few small brown spots with dark border on leaves.
treatments.action: Cut off diseased leaves and fruit clusters

treatments.identification_guide: Lesions spread to fruit stems, some fruits start withering.
treatments.action: Spray fungicide from new shoot emergence

treatments.identification_guide: The whole grape cluster is black rotted and withered.

Grape Esca.name: Grape Esca (Black Measles)
Grape Esca.desc: Disease complex caused by fungi weakening old grapevines.

treatments.identification_guide: Leaves have light yellow streaks between veins.
Leaf scorch spreads clearly in red-brown color.
treatments.action: Apply protective drug to pruning cuts

treatments.identification_guide: Branches die dry, fruit has scattered black spots.
treatments.action: Remove heavily diseased plants to avoid spreading

Grape Leaf Blight.name: Grape Leaf Blight
Grape Leaf Blight.desc: Disease caused by Pseudocercospora vitis fungus.
symptoms: Often appears at the end of the season

treatments.identification_guide: Small brown spots appear on old leaves.
Lesions enlarge and connect causing large blight area.
Mass leaf drop, affecting fruit ripening.
Glossy leaves, no tiger-stripe scorch.
treatments.action: Apply Potassium to increase fruit quality

Orange Haunglongbing.name: Citrus Greening (HLB)
Orange Haunglongbing.desc: Disease caused by Liberibacter bacteria via Asian citrus psyllid.
symptoms: Leaf veins turned green or swollen

treatments.identification_guide: A few leaves have blotchy yellowing on a few branches.
Many branches are yellowed, fruit starts dropping.
treatments.action: Supplement foliar nutrition to maintain plant strength

treatments.identification_guide: Plant completely exhausted, leaves drop, branches die dry.
treatments.action: Destroy plant to prevent spread of disease area

Peach Bacterial Spot.name: Peach Bacterial Spot
Peach Bacterial Spot.desc: Disease caused by Xanthomonas bacteria on peaches and plums.
symptoms: Small water-soaked spots on leaves, then shot-holes

treatments.identification_guide: Small light brown dots appear on the upper leaf surface.
Lesions dry up and fall out creating holes (shot-hole).
Many yellow leaves drop, fruit deeply cracked.
Healthy leaves, no tiny shot-holes from bacteria.

Pepper Bell Bacterial Spot.name: Pepper Bell Bacterial Spot
Pepper Bell Bacterial Spot.desc: Xanthomonas campestris bacteria causing spots on leaves.

treatments.identification_guide: A few small green-black spots on the back of the leaf.
Lesions spread causing leaf margin blight.
treatments.action: Spray mixture of copper and Mancozeb

treatments.identification_guide: Mass leaf drop, bare branches.
treatments.action: Remove diseased plants, avoid overhead irrigation

treatments.identification_guide: Lush leaves, no water-soaked spots.
treatments.action: Apply extra Calcium to avoid blossom end rot

Potato Early Blight.name: Potato Early Blight
Potato Early Blight.desc: Disease caused by Alternaria solani fungus causing concentric rings.
symptoms: Dark brown spots with concentric rings like a target

treatments.identification_guide: A few small brown spots with concentric rings on basal leaves.
treatments.action: Water at the base, avoid wetting leaves

treatments.identification_guide: Lesions spread to middle leaf layers.
The entire foliage is blighted dry.

Potato Late Blight.name: Potato Late Blight
Potato Late Blight.desc: Extremely dangerous disease caused by Phytophthora infestans fungus.
symptoms: White mold on underside of leaves in moist weather

treatments.identification_guide: Small water-soaked lesion at leaf margin or tip.
treatments.action: Closely monitor cool moist weather forecast

treatments.identification_guide: Lesions enlarge, faint white mold appears.
The whole potato field turns black and collapses.
Clean leaves, no concentric black rings.
treatments.action: Hill up high to protect tubers

Raspberry Healthy.name: Healthy Raspberry Leaf
symptoms: Fresh green leaves, even serrated margins
No signs of fungus or pests

treatments.identification_guide: Healthy leaves, no yellowing or brown spots.
Clean leaves, no leaf rollers or bacterial spots.

Squash Powdery Mildew.name: Squash Powdery Mildew
Squash Powdery Mildew.desc: Fungus covers leaves with a white powder layer.
symptoms: White powder layer covering leaf surface

treatments.identification_guide: Small white powder spots scattered on leaf surface.
White powder layer spreads to the whole leaf.
Leaves completely covered, brittle and blighted.

Strawberry Leaf Scorch.name: Strawberry Leaf Scorch
Strawberry Leaf Scorch.desc: Disease caused by Diplocarpon earlianum fungus.
symptoms: Leaves blighted dry as if scorched by fire

treatments.identification_guide: Small purple spots appear individually.
Lesions spread causing leaf margin dry blight.
The entire foliage is scorched, plant exhausted.

Strawberry Healthy.name: Healthy Strawberry Leaf
symptoms: Dark green leaves, even white flowers

treatments.identification_guide: Lush leaves, no margin scorch or purple spots.

Tomato Early Blight.name: Tomato Early Blight
Tomato Early Blight.desc: Disease caused by Alternaria solani fungus, creating concentric rings on leaves.
symptoms: Dark brown spots with distinct concentric rings
Old leaves wilt and drop first

treatments.identification_guide: A few small target spots on basal leaves.
Many target spots spread to middle leaf layers.
Foliage blighted more than 50%, affecting fruit.

Tomato Late Blight.name: Tomato Late Blight
Tomato Late Blight.desc: Very dangerous disease, can kill the whole tomato field after a few days.
symptoms: White mold layer on underside of leaves when moist
Dark rot on stems and fruit

treatments.identification_guide: Small water-soaked lesion starts appearing at leaf margin.
Lesions spread to stem and white mold appears.
The whole plant collapses, rotted.

Tomato Bacterial Spot.name: Tomato Bacterial Spot
symptoms: Small, hard, scabby black spots on leaves and fruit
Lesions spread quickly during heavy rain

treatments.identification_guide: Small black spots slightly scratched on the leaf back, not yet spread to leaf sheaths.
treatments.action: Use disease-free seeds, soak in warm water

treatments.identification_guide: Dense black spots, leaf margin scorching, plant slightly wilted.
Disease spreads to branches and young fruit, plant stem rots black, severe necrosis.
treatments.action: Spray mixture of Copper and Mancozeb

Tomato Septoria Leaf Spot.name: Tomato Septoria Leaf Spot
Tomato Septoria Leaf Spot.desc: Fungus causes many small spots, gray center with brown border.
symptoms: Many small circular gray-white spots with black borders
Leaves drop from base to top

treatments.identification_guide: Only a few faint gray spots scattered on a few single leaves.
Entire leaf peppered with dozens of small spots, leaf margins start curling dry.
Basal leaves all fallen, disease spread near the top, plant thin and weak.

Tomato Mosaic Virus.name: Tomato Mosaic Virus (ToMV)
Tomato Mosaic Virus.desc: Virus spreads through seeds, tools, and insects.

treatments.identification_guide: Small part of young leaves has light green - dark green mottling.
treatments.action: Wash hands with soap after touching diseased plants

treatments.identification_guide: Lá mọc ra bị vặn xoắn, có đốm vàng loang lổ khắp tán.
treatments.action: Tiêu diệt côn trùng truyền bệnh rầy, rệp

treatments.identification_guide: Plant completely deformed, thread-like leaves, cannot flower or set fruit.
treatments.action: Uproot all diseased plants, disinfect tools
treatments.product: Stop cultivating Solanaceous crops for one season

Tomato Yellow Leaf Curl Virus.name: Tomato Yellow Leaf Curl Virus (TYLCV)
Tomato Yellow Leaf Curl Virus.desc: Virus transmitted by silverleaf whiteflies, causes leaf curling and yellowing.
symptoms: Distinct yellowing at the top, young leaves become small

treatments.identification_guide: Plant tip starts turning lemon yellow, leaves slightly thickened.
Young leaves twisted tight, margins curled up like a cup, plant stops growing tall.
treatments.action: Spray mineral oil so whiteflies don't land

treatments.identification_guide: Squash tip matted and not developing, entire foliage yellowed.
treatments.action: Absolutely control whiteflies with strong drugs

Tomato Leaf Mold.name: Tomato Leaf Mold
Tomato Leaf Mold.desc: Disease caused by Fulvia fulva fungus in extremely high humidity.
symptoms: Light yellow spots on upper leaf surface, green/gray mold layer on underside
Leaves dry and drop but don't wilt

treatments.identification_guide: A few faint light yellow streaks on the upper leaf surface, flip to the underside to see fine gray fuzz.
Mold sticks thick like gray velvet on the underside of most old leaves.
Leaves dry black and fall in mass, disease spreads up to young leaves at the tip.

Tomato Two Spotted Spider Mites.name: Tomato Two-Spotted Spider Mite
Tomato Two Spotted Spider Mites.desc: Mites suck sap causing leaves to discolor and dry.
symptoms: Pale leaves, thin spider webs on underside
Leaves scorched yellow across the field

treatments.identification_guide: Leaves have a few faint tiny white dots, look closely to see small mites moving on the back.
treatments.action: Spray water strongly on the leaf underside

treatments.identification_guide: Leaves clearly silver-white, thin web on the back, leaves slightly curled.
Entire field scorched yellow, leaves dry and dead, mites cover the plant tips.
